center.name: 婆罗洲
center.summary: 婆罗洲是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联91种鸟类，包括菲律宾冢雉、Bornean crestless fireback、红头林鹧鸪等91种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: This species is a native resident of lowland forests across southern Thailand, southern Myanmar, the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, Riau Archipelago, and the islands of Banjak, Bangka, Belitung, Borneo, and Natuna. It hangs around the middle storeys of lowland semi-evergreen and evergreen forests, which include freshwater swamp forests, in lowlands and in hills. It is sometimes in the lower storeys and borders of forests, and in mature secondary growth forests but tends not to live in mangroves, in cultivated areas, or in open country. The presence of lianas is also favored by this species. The highest sightings of individuals vary depending on the location: in the Malay Peninsula, the highest sighting was at , and on Mount Kinabalu, it was at , while it was only about in Thailand. In Kalimantan, the height the birds were inhabiting varied between

birds.evidence: thumb|right|Fairy pitta in southern Thailand The fairy pitta is a migratory species and travels from Northeast Asia, where it breeds in summer (April–September) to South and Southeast Asia to spend the winter (October–March). Sightings have been reported from India and Indochina, including both the Bruneian and Kalimantan sides of the island of Borneo, and New Guinea, as well as in China, North Korea, South Korea, and Taiwan

The changeable hawk-eagle inhabits savannawoodland, agricultural land, wetlands, tea plantations, forest villages and even suburbs

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Japanese leaf warbler is a migratory bird. After breeding, it moves south or southwest through eastern China and nearby islands. It spends the winter in Taiwan, the Philippines, Borneo, and Java. Migration takes place from August to November, and the birds return to Japan in mid-May. Arrival often happens within one or two weeks, although timing can be confused with other related species

birds.label: 分布于

It sometimes visits wooded gardens near forests

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It mainly inhabits the islands of Java and Bali. A few individuals have been reported on Lombok and Sumatra islands, and in Southeastern Borneo. It mostly inhabits open woodlands, gardens and mangroves. This bird's presence has also been reported on the seacoast and in the lowlands of Sumatra Island. It is found between the sea level and up to a maximum of 600 meters in altitude. Many tourists travel to the region of Taro on the island of Bali to observe this bird.{{Cite journal|last=Ginantra, Darmadi, Gari, Sudirga|first=I Ketut, A.A

birds.evidence: thumb|A wreathed hornbill in flight The wreathed hornbill inhabits tropical evergreen forests in the region from southern Bhutan, Northeast India, Bangladesh and through mainland Southeast Asia to Indonesia, where it is restricted to Sumatra, Java, Borneo and a few smaller islands

birds.label: 分布于

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: They have a wide distribution ranging from southeastern China to the Greater Sundas Islands. The most concentrated distribution is found in Singapore, Malaysia, southern Cambodia, and southern Thailand. Other locations with greater dispersal include Borneo and Java. It is most widespread at low altitudes, between 0–670 m, but is known to occur up to 1,600 m. Their habitat includes a wide variety of flat plains, such as farmland, suburban gardens, riversides, dunes, and sandy clearings. In the winter, some blue-throated bee-eaters move to forest canopies and saltwater channels of mangrove forests. Unlike most bee-eaters which dig nest burrows in near-vertical sand cliffs, they often dig nest burrows nearly horizontally into flat ground. Colony sizes range from 50 to 200 pairs or living completely solitary in the open country

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The Japanese leaf warbler is a migratory bird. After breeding, it moves south or southwest through eastern China and nearby islands. It spends the winter in Taiwan, the Philippines, Borneo, and Java. Migration takes place from August to November, and the birds return to Japan in mid-May. Arrival often happens within one or two weeks, although timing can be confused with other related species

links.evidence: It mainly inhabits the islands of Java and Bali. A few individuals have been reported on Lombok and Sumatra islands, and in Southeastern Borneo. It is found between the sea level and up to a maximum of 600 meters in altitude. links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: They have a wide distribution ranging from southeastern China to the Greater Sundas Islands. The most concentrated distribution is found in Singapore, Malaysia, southern Cambodia, and southern Thailand. Other locations with greater dispersal include Borneo and Java. It is most widespread at low altitudes, between 0–670 m, but is known to occur up to 1,600 m. Their habitat includes a wide variety of flat plains, such as farmland, suburban gardens, riversides, dunes, and sandy clearings. In the winter, some blue-throated bee-eaters move to forest canopies and saltwater channels of mangrove forests. Unlike most bee-eaters which dig nest burrows in near-vertical sand cliffs, they often dig nest burrows nearly horizontally into flat ground. Colony sizes range from 50 to 200 pairs or living completely solitary in the open country
